summary: Full frontend UX + data-testid verification of the Kanhaiya Mehta portfolio (React CRA, no backend). Executed all 12 requested checks on desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844). All critical flows PASS: hero intro sequence (Hi... -> Welcome -> Kanhaiya Mehta), rotating roles (all 3 observed), CTAs, scroll indicator, navbar active-highlight + mobile menu, About/Achievements/Skills/Projects/Research/Contact sections, project modal (open + close via close btn / Escape / backdrop), all project links with correct hrefs, research card content + badges, contact link hrefs, no horizontal scroll on either viewport, no JS console errors. Only one minor placeholder issue found: contact-download-cv href is '#' (PROFILE.cv is set to '#').
action_items: MINOR (non-blocking): PROFILE.cv is set to '#' in /app/frontend/src/data/portfolio.js, so the 'Download CV' button in contact-download-cv does not actually download a file. Consider hosting the CV PDF (e.g., /public/kanhaiya-mehta-cv.pdf) and updating PROFILE.cv to that path. Not part of stated review checklist but user-facing.
critical_code_review_comments: Code quality is good: components are small, focused, and each has clear responsibility. Uses env-based URL properly (no backend used, so N/A). data-testid coverage is excellent across all interactive and informational elements.
Projects.jsx modal correctly disables body scroll while active and cleans up on close (Escape + backdrop + close btn all wired).
Navbar IntersectionObserver rootMargin '-40% 0px -55% 0px' produces reliable active-link switching in tests.
Minor: PROFILE.cv = '#' — placeholder CV link; will scroll to top on click due to bare '#' fragment. Consider empty download attribute + real asset or hide CTA until asset exists.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (12/12 checklist items PASS; 1 minor placeholder note)

test_credentials: N/A — purely static portfolio, no auth.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 12 review checklist items verified end-to-end on desktop + mobile. Detailed evidence (per-check): (1) hero-intro-0 text='Hi...', hero-intro-1 text='Welcome', hero-name text='Kanhaiya Mehta' after ~3.2s. (2) All 3 rotating roles observed within 6s window: Full-Stack Developer, AI/ML Engineer, Quantum Computing Researcher. (3) hero-cta-projects scrolls Projects to top (projects.getBoundingClientRect().top ~= 0). (4) hero-cta-contact scrolls Contact section into view. (5) hero-scroll-indicator visible & clickable, scrolls to About. (6) All 7 nav-link-* toggle text-brand-cyan active class on click. (7) Mobile viewport 390x844: nav-mobile-toggle visible, nav-mobile-menu appears only after toggle, mobile links scroll (nav-mobile-projects -> scrollY=5063). (8) About shows both 'IIIT Dharwad' and 'Quantum Information Science' text; about-stat-0/1/2 present. (9) All 5 achievement-card-0..4 + achievement-rank-0..4 present. (10) All 6 skill-group-0..5 present, 28 skill-chip-* elements. (11) Project cards project-card-{truthforge,enterpriseops,telemetry,ragvault,candidate-ranking} all present. TruthForge modal opens; project-modal-link-truthforge-live-demo href = https://truthforge-iota.vercel.app; project-modal-link-truthforge-github href = https://github.com/Kanhaiya76618/truthforge; modal closes via close btn, Escape, and backdrop click. projects-github-all href = https://github.com/Kanhaiya76618. (12) research-title contains 'Quantum Approximate Optimization for Join Order Selection'; all 4 research-badge-0..3 texts match required labels; research-card is NOT inside projects-section. (13) Contact hrefs all match spec (github, linkedin, mailto). contact-download-cv exists but href='#' (see action_items). (14) No horizontal scroll on desktop or mobile (scrollWidth == innerWidth). (15) Zero page errors, zero console errors (only React DevTools info log).
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Contact CV button has href='#' because PROFILE.cv is a placeholder '#' in data/portfolio.js. Reproduction: navigate to Contact section and inspect the [data-testid='contact-download-cv'] anchor. Mitigation: place the CV PDF under /app/frontend/public/ (e.g., cv.pdf) and set PROFILE.cv to '/cv.pdf' in /app/frontend/src/data/portfolio.js.
